**Department Overview**
The AI Services team creates solutions by applying our experience in workflow development, system integrations, and infrastructure deployment. Our products leverage cutting edge machine learning capabilities to enable our customers to focus on providing faster responses, safer outcomes, and greater transparency. You will join a talented engineering organization with a track record of delivering meaningful and impactful solutions.##
[...]
The AI Services team is looking for an experienced software engineer experienced with audio data, machine learning, and designing scalable systems. Our area of work is to enable fast, accurate, and natural communication between human operators and their underlying information in real-time systems.
[...]
* Develop mission-critical software solutions that are maintainable, testable, scalable, performant, and highly available.
* Promote best practices in software architecture, coding standards, and quality assurance while developing interfaces and workflow orchestration for real-time and batched solutions.
* Support evaluation and analysis of our solutions and models (latency benchmarks, noisy audio and data, accuracy, etc) 
* Build and warehouse datasets for training and validation purposes, working closely with data scientists and other engineers.
* Ensure security, compliance, and compartmentalization of data for public safety and enterprise customers.
* Design and implement scalable ML/AI systems and pipeline
* Optimize model performance and accuracy to meet system requirements
[...]
* 4+ years of software development experience working with Python, SQL
[...]
* Highly proficient database and data modeling skills
* Experience developing containerized solutions (Docker, Kubernetes) and serverless infrastructure (e.g. AWS Lambda).
[...]
* Experience working with cloud-based solutions and configuration: Azure, AWS, and GCP.
[...]
* Excellent written and oral communication skills to collaborate in a multinational environment with teams spanning the globe.
* Familiarity with MLOps and full ML lifecycle management from prototype to production
* Experience iterating on models and production code based on feedback
**Additional Skills**
[...]
* Experience working with ML models, including traditional networks and large language models (LLMs)
* Delivered solutions across domains like audio, NLP, generative AI, edge AI
* Experience with ML frameworks (PyTorch, Tensorflow) and infrastructure (e.g., Databricks, MLFlow, AWS SageMaker, Hugging Face) 
* Experience with distributed computing frameworks like Ray
* Experience with orchestration tools like Apache Airflow or Temporal
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
[...]
* Bachelors degree with 4+ years of software development experience working with Python
* AND 4+ years of experience using CI/CD (Github Actions, Azure DevOps, or Jenkins)
* Legal authorization to work in the U.S. indefinitely is required. Employer work permit sponsorship is not available for this position.
* Must be a U.S. citizen with ability to obtain necessary security clearance as required by government contract

# Software Engineer II

Posted May 25, 2026

Motorola Solutions is a global community focused on building technologies that enhance public safety. They are seeking a Software Engineer II to analyze, develop, design, and maintain software for their products, contributing to the full product lifecycle in an agile team environment.

### Responsibilities

- Analyzes, develops, designs, and maintains software for the organization’s products and systems
- Contributes to the full product lifecycle by performing software development, executing hardware and software integration tests, and developing and validating all feature functionality and user documentation
- Candidates are expected to work in a team environment, using the latest industry-standard agile practices to plan, implement, and deliver products and services

Skills

- Bachelor's (BS) or Master's (MS) degree in Computer Science, Electrical or Computer Engineering or related technical discipline
- 1 to 2 years of relevant embedded system software development experience
- US Citizenship required. Employer work permit sponsorship is not available for this position
- C/C++
- Linux (multiple variants)
- Windows development
- Python, Perl / Powershell
- Java
- Make, Bash
- Network Protocols (TCP/IP, TLS, UDP)
- Agile software development and/or Git/JIRA experience is a plus
- Mobile application and/or platform development, including Android and iOS
- Driver development
- Embedded programming
- SIP, RTP, RTCP Protocols
- Experience in debugging and strong analytical skills
- Security concepts such as authentication, access control, authorization, and cryptography
- Strong communication and problem-solving skills
- Knowledge of Gen AI and LLM is a plus

### Benefits

- Incentive Bonus Plans
- Medical, Dental, Vision benefits
- 401K with Company Match
- 10 Paid Holidays
- Generous Paid Time Off Packages
- Employee Stock Purchase Plan
- Paid Parental & Family Leave
- And more!

Company Overview

- Motorola Solutions creates mission-critical communication solutions and services for public safety and commercial customers. It was founded in 1928, and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.motorolasolutions.com.

# Full Stack .NET Developer
[...]
The Full Stack .NET Developer will design, develop, and maintain internal applications using .NET technologies, support users, and ensure security compliance.
[...]
Department OverviewOur Applied Technology organization puts some of Motorola Solutions' brightest minds on unique, highly challenging projects. Based on decades of experience with our customers, we apply leading-edge technologies to support security and safety throughout the world. We engineer our products from start to finish and manufacture them in-house. We're known for low volume and high capability with focused specialization in areas including software-defined radios, advanced antenna solutions, artificial intelligence and machine learning, cybersecurity, signals intelligence and more. We can't tell you much more about what we do, but we can tell you it's amazing.Job Description
[...]
We are looking for an ambitious, highly versatile Full Stack .NET Developer to join our close-knit internal business tools team. Because we operate as a small, agile group, you will wear many hats and have a hand in every phase of the software development lifecycle. You won't just be writing code, you will sit down with end-users to gather requirements, manage daily Helpdesk tickets from our internal customers, write documentation, deploy custom tools, ensure strict security standards, and even help maintain our web and SQL servers.
[...]
Your primary focus will be developing brand-new internal tools using the latest .NET technologies (leveraging AI coding assistants to work smarter!), while also migrating and maintaining a handful of legacy .NET Framework/MVC applications as time permits. Additionally, you'll help us integrate and extend enterprise Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) applications. If you love variety, enjoy end-to-end ownership of your projects, and want to see the direct impact of your work on our business groups, this is the perfect role for you.
[...]
Full Lifecycle Development: Design, develop, and deploy modern custom internal applications using the latest .NET technologies, leveraging AI coding assistants and prompt engineering to accelerate development and enhance code quality. Occasionally maintain legacy .NET Framework/ASP.NET tools.
[...]
We know that the best candidates don't always check every box. If you are excited about this role and have a solid foundation in .NET development, we strongly encourage you to apply. Many of
[...]
specialized skills below can be learned on the job!
[...]
Strong experience in full-stack web development, heavily focused on Microsoft .NET technologies (C#, latest .NET / .NET Core, ASP.NET).
[...]
Experience utilizing AI coding assistants and prompt engineering to streamline development workflows.
[...]
Experience integrating COTS applications
[...]
prior experience with Deltek Costpoint and/or Aras Innovator is a massive plus).

Bachelor's of Science degree in Computer Science or related field
[...]
5+ years of professional software development experience

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD and Roche to solve
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
in machine learning
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning

Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI) today announced the opening of its new AI and resilience software hub in Boston, Massachusetts. The hub serves as a key research and development site for applied AI and mission-critical, cloud-based solutions, powering the core platform that enables seamless emergency coordination and collaboration between private enterprises and first responders. Thousands of K-12 schools and higher education institutions rely on this platform to manage complex incidents with clarity and speed.

Deepening our expertise in Boston allows us to more effectively bridge the gap between complex data streams and meaningful action to better protect critical enterprises and communities.”

results.title: Motorola Solutions opens AI and resilience software hub in Boston
highlights:
Motorola Solutions opens AI and resilience software hub in Boston

Back to: All AI News

# Motorola Solutions opens AI and resilience software hub in Boston

Motorola Solutions is opening an AI and cloud software R&D hub in Boston focused on emergency management tools for schools, hospitals, and public agencies. The company plans to hire in AI research, engineering, and product management.

Categorized in: AI News Product Development

Published on: May 25, 2026

Become an AI Expert today

## Motorola Solutions Opens Boston AI Hub for Emergency Management Software

Motorola Solutions is opening a research and development hub in Boston focused on AI and cloud-based software for emergency response. The facility will develop technologies that help schools, hospitals, and public sector organizations manage crises more efficiently.

The company said the hub will accelerate work on its safety platform, which thousands of K-12 schools and colleges currently use to coordinate emergency situations. Boston's location strengthens Motorola Solutions' existing Massachusetts operations and signals expanded investment in AI-driven public safety tools.

Motorola Solutions plans to hire across AI research, software engineering, and product management at the new facility. The company is developing what it calls "agentic systems"-AI tools designed to manage emergencies at scale by converting complex data into actionable intelligence for first responders and enterprise security teams.

### Why Boston

Jehan Wickramasuriya, senior vice president of security and resilience software, said Boston's innovation ecosystem made it the natural choice. "The problems we're solving are some of the most challenging and rewarding in any field," he said, adding that the company seeks talent interested in deploying emergency management systems at scale.

Mahesh Saptharishi, chief technology officer, framed the hub as a bridge between data analysis and real-world action. "We're delivering actionable intelligence for those who manage society's most complex security challenges," he said.

### What This Means for Product Development

For product development professionals, this expansion reflects how organizations are embedding AI into mission-critical workflows. The focus on converting data streams into usable intelligence for time-sensitive decisions mirrors challenges across industries beyond emergency response.

Motorola Solutions' emphasis on agentic systems suggests product teams should understand how autonomous AI agents can operate within existing organizational structures-a technical and organizational design problem that extends well beyond public safety.

Learn more about AI for Product Development and Generative AI and LLM technologies shaping how teams build AI-driven products.

Motorola Solutions is a company that provides a comprehensive safety and security ecosystem, offering technology solutions to protect people, property, and places. The company specializes in critical communications, video security systems, command center solutions, and managed & support services. Their products include mission-critical P25 radios, professional DMR radios, broadband devices, and video security systems powered by AI analytics. Motorola Solutions serves various industries, including public safety, education, healthcare, law enforcement, and more, with a focus on enhancing operational efficiency and response in critical situations. They are committed to delivering solutions that help their customers stay connected, communicate clearly, and act with certainty in the harshest conditions.

results.title: Applications Engineer-I, USA | Hirevector
highlights:
Applications Engineer-I, USA | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Applications Engineer-I, USA

Posted May 29, 2026

Motorola Solutions is a global community focused on safety and security technologies. They are seeking an Applications Engineer-I to conduct technical activities related to applications engineering and customer support, working closely with customers and the sales team to drive product development and solutions.

### Responsibilities

- Design and test complete solutions that solve unique technical problems
- Define new products and features to fill gaps and perform product evaluations and field trials for new product features and accessories
- Provide comprehensive support to customers and the sales team through troubleshooting, system integration engineering, product recommendations, and technical analysis via phone, online, and in person
- Participate in an off-hour, on-call support rotation (on a voluntary or scheduled basis as business needs require) to address critical customer escalations
- Work individually and with Engineering teams to support requests via in-house or through field testing and validation
- Test and harden existing features through firmware and hardware changes in realistic applications
- Support the Sales Engineering team by helping prepare for demonstrations, training, testing, or recreating bugs and issues seen in the field
- Create and maintain customer-facing documents and content including manuals, guides, videos, application notes, etc. to support customers and sales more efficiently
- Oversee and assist the RMA and Loaner teams by providing guidance, equipment recommendations, and vetting customer requests
- Travel requirements – Up to 25% travel to provide in-person engineering support to customers and the sales team, including demos, troubleshooting, system integration, and training
- Perform other related duties, of which the above are representative

Skills

- Bachelor of Science degree in Electrical Engineering, Engineering Technology, or related technical field
- Proficiency with instruments such as spectrum analyzers, multimeters, signal generators, and other radio frequency and data network test equipment and testing
- Must possess an ability to work independently, take initiative, multitask, and effectively interact with cross-functional teams and customers
- Strong verbal and written communication skills are a must to effectively communicate with customers and internally across the entire organization
- Excellent organizational skills and attentiveness to details
- Proficiency with PC office applications and mobile devices
- Willingness to travel and work at customer sites
- Must be a U.S. Person (U.S. Citizen, or U.S. Permanent Resident) due to clients under U.S. federal contracts
- All employment is contingent upon the successful clearance of a background check and drug test
- Proficiency with TCP/IP protocol and networking
- Experience with operating and testing IP radios/modems
- Knowledge of wireless communication systems, radio frequency signal propagation, and digital communication technologies
- Experience with testing embedded radio communication systems
- Familiarity with Linux
- Proficiency with programming languages such as Python or C/C++

### Benefits

- Incentive Bonus Plans
- Medical, Dental, Vision benefits
- 401K with Company Match
- 10 Paid Holidays
- Generous Paid Time Off Packages
- Employee Stock Purchase Plan
- Paid Parental & Family Leave
- And more!

Company Overview

- Motorola Solutions creates mission-critical communication solutions and services for public safety and commercial customers. It was founded in 1928, and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.motorolasolutions.com.

Apply for This Job

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Hirevector
highlights:
Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Hirevector
[...]
# Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II
[...]
We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS.
[...]
- End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach(prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy.
[...]
- Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference.
[...]
- Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation.
[...]
- Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails.
[...]
- Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration).
[...]
- MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies.
[...]
- Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs.
[...]
- Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs.
[...]
- Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments.
[...]
### Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities)
[...]
- 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems.
- MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
[...]
- Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals.
- Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents.
[...]
- Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA).
[...]
- Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services).
[...]
- Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale.
[...]
- Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses.
[...]
- Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems.
[...]
- Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM.
[...]
- Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana).
[...]
- Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral).
[...]
- Languages: Python, SQL
- Data: Snowflake, Postgres
- Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda)
- MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B
[...]
- Xsolis is an AI-driven technology company with a human-centered approach, fostering collaboration between healthcare providers and payers through real-time transparency, objective data for increased accuracy and alignment of medical necessity decisions, and more efficient outcomes. Dragonfly®, its AI-driven proprietary platform, is the first and only solution to use real-time predictive analytics to continuously assign an objective medical necessity score and assess the anticipated level of care for every patient, enabling more efficiency across the healthcare system. Xsolis is headquartered in Franklin, Tennessee.

highlights: in the moments that matter - and we live up to our purpose every day by solving for safer. Because people can only be their best when they not only feel safe, but are safe. We're solving for safer by building the best possible technologies across every part of our safety and security ecosystem. That's mission-critical communications devices and networks, AI-powered video security & access control and the ability to unite voice, video and data in a single command center view. We're solving for safer by connecting public safety agencies and enterprises, enabling the collaboration that's critical to connect those in need with those who can help. The work we do here matters.Department OverviewThe Data Protection Counsel - Information Security serves as a member of the Motorola Solutions, Inc. Data Protection - Products team and a subject matter expert on privacy and security compliance. In addition, this role will serve as legal and compliance advisor to the Market Readiness and Customer Due Diligence Response Teams in the development and maturity of their Market Readiness security and privacy maturity and Customer Information Security Due Diligence Response Programs. While the Data Protection Counsel will report to the Sr. Privacy Counsel, Information Security and Services, he/she will also work closely and collaboratively with the services organization, internal product, engineering, legal, cybersecurity, compliance operations and business teams.Job DescriptionResponsibilities:Serve as data protection subject matter legal and compliance expert to cybersecurity, legal, service and global product teams. Provide advice and counsel on relevant global privacy and security laws, standards and policies. Assist and advise commercial counsel with the evaluation and negotiation of data protection contract terms and conditions.Determine applicability and obligations of privacy and security regulations and frameworks.Participate in cybersecurity and privacy assessments of Motorola Solutions, products and services to advise on the privacy and security compliance implications and develop ways to mitigate compliance risks.Provide guidance to internal teams with respect to data flow mapping and compilation of data and asset inventories.Provide Legal analysis in connection with data protection impact assessments and records of processing activities. Provide proactive and creative legal guidance on all privacy and data protection compliance risks in connection with the launch of new services. results.title: Financial Analyst | Hirequorum
highlights:
Financial Analyst | Hirequorum

Hirequorum.← All Jobs

# Financial Analyst

Posted Jun 3, 2026

Motorola Solutions is a global community focused on safety and security technologies. Silvus Technologies, part of Motorola, is seeking a detail-oriented Financial Analyst to support the FP&A team by providing quality reporting, optimizing processes, and assisting with strategic financial projects.

### Responsibilities

- Support strategic initiatives and analyses across the business by gathering and preparing data for cross-functional teams
- Assist in performing variance analysis by diving into financial data to help identify the root causes behind the numbers
- Prepare clear summaries of financial information and key insights for review by senior team members
- Support management in tracking and monitoring performance against relevant Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
- Generate ad hoc reports and analyses as requested by senior leadership
- Support data integrity and validation efforts within FP&A, learning to work with financial systems
- Assist in building and maintaining transformation files and financial models used to structure and clean raw data
- Learn and utilize advanced tools like Alteryx and Power Query for data manipulation and process optimization
- Develop and maintain complex financial models to support strategic planning, budgeting, and forecasting
- Assist in preparing monthly, quarterly, and annual variance reports

Skills

- Bachelor's degree or higher in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related quantitative field
- Up to 2 years of experience as a financial analyst or related role
- Demonstrated ability to approach tasks with a high degree of attention to detail and organizational skills
- Experience with and a desire to learn advanced data manipulation in tools like Excel, Alteryx, and/or Power Query
- Strong foundational analytical mindset with the ability to look deeply into numbers and identify patterns
- Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively with others across teams to resolve data and process issues
- Must be a U.S. Person (U.S. Citizen, or U.S. Permanent Resident) due to clients under U.S. federal contracts
- All employment is contingent upon the successful clearance of a background check and drug test
- Familiarity with other data manipulation tools and languages such as SQL or Python
- Relevant professional certification or progress towards one (e.g., CPA or CMA)
- Any exposure to integrated three financial statement modeling
- Familiarity with financial systems integration and data architecture

### Benefits

- Medical, Dental, Vision benefits
- 401K with Company Match

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects.
